mrca_with_hunter.name: Jesse Dayton Herron
mrca_with_hunter.notes: Jesse Dayton Herron b.1918 is a confirmed shared ancestor for 4scherff, Burton, Gary Burton. His connection to Hunter's line is via the William Woodville Herron family. The Herron cluster appears to connect to Hunter's paternal line via Alabama/Texas Stuart cousins, but the exact MRCA between Herron and Hunter's direct line is not yet document-confirmed.

cousins.evidence: 'Jesse Dayton Herron' found at depth 2 above cousin in GEDCOM. Matches Hunter anchor 'Jesse Dayton Herron' (b.1918) on paternal side via Stuart line.

'Spence' not found in GEDCOM despite filename labeling paternal side. No Henslee, Stuart, Byrd, or other Hunter anchors found. Paternal side labeling may be incorrect. The Rau surname in Hunter's line (Frances Virginia Rau b.1918) is distinct from the French Perrault/Perault line here. Cannot verify without additional DNA evidence.

cousins.evidence: Byrd surname present — ancestors include Martha Ann Byrd b.1665 and Walter Chiles b.1607-1630 (colonial Virginia), who appear as deep ancestors in other verified cousins' trees (Grace Chewning). However Frances Padgett's NEAR ancestors (d=1-3) are in Arkansas/Indiana with no Henslee/Stuart/Byrd match in Hunter's known line. Connection if any is likely via a colonial Virginia colonial ancestor 5+ generations removed. Cannot confirm MRCA without more research.

mrca_with_hunter.name: William Woodville Herron
mrca_with_hunter.notes: b.1885 Union Grove Marshall Alabama. Shared ancestor confirmed via multiple cousin GEDCOMs. Probable connection to Hunter via a shared pre-Civil-War ancestor in the Herron/Stuart/Weed network of Burleson County TX / Alabama families. The exact generational link between William Woodville Herron and Hunter's direct Stuart line (Mary Alice Stuart b.1887 Caldwell Burleson TX) requires further research — they may share Lewis Lunsford Stuart b.1830 or an earlier common ancestor.

cousins.cousin_file: Grace Chewning - Half 2nd cousin 2x removed or 2nd cousin 3x removed - unassigned.ged
cousins.claimed_relationship: Half 2nd cousin 2x removed or 2nd cousin 3x removed - unassigned
cousins.evidence: Large GEDCOM (1,293 people). 'Rau' surname appears but as a different European Rau, not Hunter's Frances Virginia Rau (1918 NY-heritage). 'Spence' found but as Spencer medieval royalty (Catherine Spencer b.1477), not Hunter's Spence immigrant line. Near ancestors (d=1-3) are Mecklenburg/Warren County Virginia/NC families (Winn, Newton, Wootton, Cliborne). Deep ancestors include colonial Virginia Chiles line (Susannah Chiles b.1724, Walter Chiles b.1607) — same Walter Chiles appears in Steven Stuart and Jess Stuart verified GEDCOMs, suggesting possible shared colonial ancestor 8-10 generations back. Unassigned in filename — likely paternal via Chiles/Stuart.
cousins.side_note: Unassigned in filename. Probable paternal colonial connection via Chiles/Stuart line ~8+ generations back.

cousins.cousin_file: Walls Doris-1st cousin 2x removed or half great grandaunt maternal side.ged
cousins.claimed_relationship: 1st cousin 2x removed or half great grandaunt maternal side
cousins.evidence: Small GEDCOM (13 people). Iris June Westerfield b.1919 (mother of @I1@ Andrew Jackson Key b.1914). Westerfield also appears in Al wood GEDCOM (O.C. Wes Westerfield b.1921 AR). Padgett surname (Bertie Jane Padgett b.1888 AR) also appears in Walls Doris tree — William Miller Padgett b.1862 is the same person in Frances Padgett's tree. These Westerfield/Padgett families cluster in Jackson/Lawrence counties Arkansas. No direct Hunter anchor. Labeled maternal side. May connect via a shared pre-1860 ancestor in the Padgett/Westerfield network.
cousins.side_note: Labeled maternal. Westerfield+Padgett overlap with Al wood and Frances Padgett GEDCOMs suggests a shared cluster.
